narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This direct quote provides a severe and derogatory criticism of Western (specifically American) society, portraying it as morally corrupt and inferior, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly accuses Western media of presenting a false narrative about the war's progress, thereby undermining its reliability and suggesting it is prolonging conflict, which fits the 'Distrust towards Media' definition.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote explicitly invokes the specter of nuclear war and total annihilation, using fatalistic language to evoke fear and anxiety about extreme consequences, which is the core of the 'Amplifying war-related fears' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, in the context of the preceding text blaming un-elected Western bureaucrats, implies that Western populations will face severe negative outcomes (presumably from the conflict) as a deserved consequence, highlighting adverse outcomes for the West.

In the context of the interview where Ritter is presented as explaining 'what is really going on' contrary to Western media, and the text frames Western media as propaganda, praising Ritter for 'talking the truth' implicitly praises the alternative (pro-Russian) perspective he represents.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This direct quote provides a severe and derogatory criticism of Western (specifically American) society, portraying it as morally corrupt and inferior, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly accuses Western media of misrepresenting the war's progress ('framing'), implying it is unreliable propaganda that has harmful real-world consequences, fitting the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote explicitly invokes the specter of nuclear war and mass annihilation, using hyperbolic and fatalistic language to evoke fear and anxiety about extreme conflict outcomes.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, in the context of the preceding text blaming Western governments and populations, implies that Western populations will face severe negative consequences (presumably from the conflict) as a form of deserved retribution.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA ... way out of proportion
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote shifts blame for the conflict and historical tensions away from Russia by portraying it as an innocent party unfairly demonized and provoked by an aggressive, exaggerating West ('amerika').

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western media for its portrayal of the war, suggesting it is prolonging conflict and causing harm, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting Western institutions and their role in diplomacy and information.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' directly undermining its reliability and integrity, which is the core of the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This is a severe and derogatory criticism of Americans, attacking the character of the Western populace, which fits the broader narrative of discrediting the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement explicitly invokes the fear of a full-scale nuclear war and the annihilation it would bring, which is a clear example of amplifying war-related fears and anxieties.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote blames unelected Western bureaucrats for leading humanity towards ruin, highlighting a negative outcome and moral failure attributed to Western governance structures.
